texto: The men laugh at the witty line, but it is not a belly laugh. Beasley's mouth is in a wide smile, but his eyes do not laugh, for there is little reason for joy. And if eyes are indeed mirrors of the soul, then they reflect an infinite sadness. I look away, afraid of what mine might reflect

And if eyes are indeed mirrors of the soul, then they reflect an infinite sadness. I look away, afraid of what mine might reflect

texto: Prison is a second-by-second assault on the soul, a day-to-day degradation of the self, an oppressive steel and brick umbrella that transforms seconds into hours and hours into days.
explicacao: Citação de Mumia Abu-Jamal sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Prison is a second-by-second assault on the soul, a day-to-day degradation of the self, an oppres…” articula reflexao, cages, oppression em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e oppression.

frase_original: Prison is a second-by-second assault on the soul, a day-to-day degradation of the self, an oppressive steel and brick umbrella that transforms seconds into hours and hours into days.

texto: Conventional wisdom would have one believe that it is insane to resist this, the mightiest of empires, but what history really shows is that today's empire is tomorrow's ashes; that nothing lasts forever, and that to not resist is to acquiesce in your own oppression. The greatest form of sanity that anyone can exercise is to resist that force that is trying to repress, oppress, and fight down the human spirit.
explicacao: Citação de Mumia Abu-Jamal sobre temas ligados a tempo. “Conventional wisdom would have one believe that it is insane to resist this, the mightiest of emp…” articula tempo, america, emancipation em registro de tempo, com relevância para contextos como emancipation e freedom.
